audit_metadata.scope: AIStock source code (src/), config files, reports, dependencies
audit_metadata.methodology: Static analysis via grep/pattern matching + manual source review

vulnerabilities.description: DeepSeek API key hardcoded as plaintext: sk-96c8c56a71f64942bf5c6630bd8a9924. Live production key, not a placeholder.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove from config.yaml. Use DEEPSEEK_API_KEY env var. config.yaml IS in .gitignore but key may already be in git history. Rotate key immediately.

vulnerabilities.description: Alpha Vantage API key hardcoded: FQZDH795B6P1JXSU. Live key exposed in plaintext.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Use ALPHA_VANTAGE_API_KEY env var. Rotate key. fetcher/__init__.py:8 reads from config dict — change to read from env instead.

vulnerabilities.description: Google API key hardcoded: AIzaSyBWRCPmYpHwI_yF3MHWMCrmMe2A92-QkbU. Duplicated at lines 9 (common) and 18 (google section). Also leaked into 10+ reports/full_pipeline/*/effective_config.yaml files.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Use GOOGLE_API_KEY env var (already read at ingestion/pipeline.py:56). Remove hardcoded value. Rotate key.

vulnerabilities.description: MySQL database credentials in connection URL: mysql+pymysql://stockdb:stockdb123@localhost/stockdb. Username/password in plaintext.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Use DATABASE_URL env var. Change password from stockdb123 to strong random value.

vulnerabilities.description: 10+ pipeline run directories contain effective_config.yaml with ALL API keys and DB credentials in plaintext. Files are untracked but NOT gitignored. Accidental git add reports/ would expose all secrets.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Add reports/ to .gitignore. Sanitize config dump in src/pipeline/config.py:39 to redact api_key/password/secret fields. Purge existing report files with secrets.

vulnerabilities.description: XSS via unsafe_allow_html=True with DB values. stock.get('name'), stock.get('exchange'), stock.get('sector'), stock.get('industry') interpolated into HTML via f-string. If DB compromised with malicious HTML, XSS executes in admin browser.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Replace unsafe_allow_html with Streamlit-safe components: st.container() + st.metric() instead of raw HTML metric cards.
vulnerabilities.exploit_scenario: Attacker with DB write access inserts <script> into company name → renders in Streamlit dashboard

vulnerabilities.description: XSS via unsafe_allow_html=True with f-string financial data (close price, change). Numeric DB values — lower direct risk but still HTML injection surface.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Use st.metric() component instead of raw HTML.

vulnerabilities.description: XSS via unsafe_allow_html=True — PE ratio f-string interpolation.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Use st.metric() component.

vulnerabilities.description: XSS via unsafe_allow_html=True — market cap f-string interpolation.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Use st.metric() component.

vulnerabilities.description: XSS via unsafe_allow_html=True — volume f-string interpolation.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Use st.metric() component.

vulnerabilities.description:
unsafe_allow_html=True for static
tag. Unnecessary HTML injection surface.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Replace with st.divider() or empty st.write('').

vulnerabilities.description: Unsafe deserialization via joblib.load() (uses pickle internally). Model files loaded from glob without integrity verification. Attacker who can write to models/ directory gets RCE.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Add HMAC signature verification on model files before loading. Consider safetensors or ONNX format. Validate file path is within expected directory and check artifact structure.
vulnerabilities.exploit_scenario: Malicious .pkl file placed in models/ → next pipeline run executes arbitrary Python code

vulnerabilities.description: Second instance of unsafe joblib.load() — _load_models_and_features(). Same RCE vector as SEC-012.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Same as SEC-012 — add integrity verification before deserialization.

vulnerabilities.description: unsafe_allow_html=True for CSS injection. Static content (low risk) but normalizes insecure pattern across codebase.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Inject CSS via Streamlit's st.markdown() without unsafe_allow_html, or use external CSS file.

vulnerabilities.description: Branding header uses unsafe_allow_html=True. Static content, low direct risk.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Use st.title() or st.header() instead of raw HTML.

vulnerabilities.description: 8 instances of unsafe_allow_html=True for tab headers across stock_lookup.py:25, stock_technical.py:21, stock_screener.py:37, stock_manager.py:41, job_history.py:15. Static content, but unnecessary HTML injection surface.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Replace all tab-header unsafe_allow_html with st.subheader() or styled st.markdown() without HTML.

vulnerabilities.description: Tab header unsafe_allow_html — part of 8-instance pattern (SEC-016).
vulnerabilities.remediation: Same as SEC-016.

vulnerabilities.description: Direct os.environ assignment: os.environ[_env_key] = _common[_cfg_key]. Writes API keys from config into process environment. Keys leak via subprocess inheritance and error tracebacks.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Pass API keys as function parameters. If env vars required by third-party libs, use temporary context manager that restores original values.

vulnerabilities.description: Second os.environ injection — _inject_api_keys() writes 6 API keys to process environment.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Same as SEC-018.

vulnerabilities.description: Third os.environ injection — _inject_api_keys() duplicates same pattern in fast evaluator backend.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Same as SEC-018.

vulnerabilities.description: Streamlit application has no authentication. Settings page accepts API keys but does not validate or persist securely. App accessible to anyone who can reach host:port.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Add Streamlit-Authenticator or OAuth proxy (nginx + oauth2-proxy) before production deployment. Document risk for local use.

sql_injection_assessment.details: All SQL queries use SQLAlchemy text() with bound parameters (:param syntax). No string concatenation or f-string SQL construction found in repository.py, ingestion/pipeline.py, or ingestion/indicators.py.

recommendations: IMMEDIATE: Rotate ALL exposed API keys — DeepSeek, Alpha Vantage, Google. Keys appear in git history and 10+ report files.
IMMEDIATE: Rotate MySQL database password. Replace stockdb123 with strong random password.
IMMEDIATE: Add reports/ to .gitignore to prevent credential leakage via effective_config.yaml.
IMMEDIATE: Sanitize effective_config.yaml generation (pipeline/config.py:39) to redact api_key, password, secret, url fields.
HIGH: Replace all 11 unsafe_allow_html=True calls with safe Streamlit components (st.metric, st.container, st.write).
HIGH: Add HMAC signature verification before joblib.load() in finrl_runner.py:149 and :359.
HIGH: Replace os.environ injection with explicit API key passing. Use context manager for third-party lib env vars.
HIGH: Run git filter-repo/BFG to scrub config.yaml from git history if tracked.
MEDIUM: Add Streamlit authentication before production deployment (streamlit-authenticator or OAuth proxy).
MEDIUM: Run pip-audit or safety check on requirements.txt for vulnerable dependency versions.
LOW: Add bandit (bandit -r src/) to CI pipeline for ongoing static security analysis.
LOW: Add pre-commit hook for secret detection (detect-secrets or git-secrets).
LOW: Add data/ to .gitignore to prevent accidental commit of cached API responses.
PROCESS: Implement quarterly API key rotation policy.
PROCESS: Never store real credentials in config files — use YOUR_KEY_HERE placeholders in docs/examples.
